title: Kissing Your Sister

jokes.text: Alabama first in the alphabet, last in every other fucking thing.
jokes.setup: Roll call vote beginning with Alabama
jokes.punchline: Brutal takedown of Alabama as worst at everything except alphabetical order
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational comedy with perfect economy. Classic Veep political incorrectness delivered with surgical precision.

jokes.text: I believe it's number one in easiest state to get away with murdering a black guy.
jokes.setup: Following up on Alabama being 'last in everything'
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark racial observation about Alabama's justice system
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that relies heavily on shock value rather than craft. Follows the Alabama setup but less elegant.

jokes.text: Ooh, bam-a-lama ding dong!
jokes.setup: Alabama voting for Selina
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'Alabama' with nonsense musical phrase
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay joke, moderately clever but not particularly original or character-driven.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, this feels like my sister took a shit on my chest.
jokes.setup: Bowling coach saying tie is like kissing your sister
jokes.punchline: Escalating metaphor to grotesque scatological imagery
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of familiar metaphor with shocking imagery. Classic Selina vulgarity that's earned through character frustration.

jokes.text: I do not give my consent to have my likeness appear in this or any other film.
jokes.setup: Catherine's documentary filming
jokes.punchline: Gary's paranoid legal disclaimer delivered formally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary character moment - anxious, formal, paranoid. Shows his protective instincts through comedy.

jokes.text: I'm gonna have to have approval over all of this footage, Catherine, okay?
jokes.setup: Catherine making documentary about the tie
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately trying to control narrative
jokes.explanation: Pure Selina character comedy - can't let anything exist without her control, even her daughter's project.

jokes.text: Daddy did a lot of business with Bebe Rebozo.
jokes.setup: Describing meeting Nixon at inaugural ball
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning father's ties to Nixon's corrupt associate
jokes.explanation: Subtle historical reference joke that reveals Selina's corrupt family background through casual namedrop.

jokes.text: I went home with a congressional fan girl last night. I don't have my car. I think I'm in Maryland.
jokes.setup: Jonah being asked where he is during crucial vote
jokes.punchline: Admitting to hookup and being geographically lost
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah irresponsibility - missing crucial vote for sex, then admitting it casually.

jokes.text: Shh! She's got a fucking parrot, and if the parrot wakes up, the parrot's gonna make noise and wake up her mom.
jokes.setup: Jonah explaining why he needs to be quiet
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous chain of parrot-related consequences
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation of consequences. The parrot detail is wonderfully specific and ridiculous.

jokes.text: I don't know how to drop a fucking pin.
jokes.setup: Being told to drop pin in Apple Maps
jokes.punchline: Technology incompetence during crisis
jokes.explanation: Simple tech incompetence joke that fits Jonah's character but isn't particularly innovative.

jokes.text: Playing catcher is kind of like being the economy czar. There's not a lot of glory, but a lot of squatting. A lot of asses in your face.
jokes.setup: Comparing economy czar position to baseball catcher
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor focusing on degrading aspects
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor that works on multiple levels. Well-structured with good escalation to crude imagery.

jokes.text: Cheese, please, Louise.
jokes.setup: Random interjection during meeting
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical rhyming phrase
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that doesn't connect to character or situation. Random humor without purpose.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, like one of those movies for people who like to be sad.
jokes.setup: Learning Catherine is making documentary
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description of documentary films
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about documentary films that fits Tom's slightly condescending character.

jokes.text: You know, at night when I dare to close my eyes, I dream about your mother losing and then coming upon her holding a cardboard sign at the bottom of a highway exit ramp.
jokes.setup: Asked about the electoral tie
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy about Selina's homeless downfall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark fantasy that reveals Bill's deep resentment. Vivid imagery and perfect character integration.

jokes.text: It says, 'I'm so sorry, Bill Ericsson.'
jokes.setup: Catherine asking what the cardboard sign says
jokes.punchline: Sign is apology specifically to him
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that makes the revenge fantasy personal. Shows Bill's need for vindication and apology.

jokes.text: When you have those kinds of negative feelings, you just pick them up and you stuff them in your box and you close it down tight and you take that box and you shove it way back here in your... in the corner of your head and swallow that key.
jokes.setup: Catherine asking about losing the presidency
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor for unhealthy emotional suppression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character revelation of Selina's psychological dysfunction disguised as motherly advice. Perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: Oh. I just think they ran out of money. They should probably just print more. I don't know why it's been such a big issue.
jokes.setup: Asked about financial crisis
jokes.punchline: Childishly simplistic solution to complex economic problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurdly naive economic theory. Sue's literal-minded character shines through.

jokes.text: Yeah, right after the inauguration, we'll show Mr. McShittock the door.
jokes.setup: Discussing firing Mike
jokes.punchline: Crude nickname combining Mike's incompetence with scatological humor
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective nickname that captures both Amy's mean streak and Mike's incompetence.

jokes.text: You need to be quick and you need to sort of, uh, I don't even know what the word is.
jokes.setup: Mike describing qualities needed for his job
jokes.punchline: Can't think of the word while describing needing to be quick
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - his incompetence demonstrated through his inability to complete his own thought.

jokes.text: And Ta-da! We went with a Winnie the Pooh knockoff. It's made in China so Ellen will feel right at home. I think he's called Happy Sun Bear.
jokes.setup: Showing off nursery for Chinese adoption
jokes.punchline: Accidentally racist logic about Chinese-made knockoff products
jokes.explanation: Classic Dan obliviousness to his own casual racism while trying to be thoughtful about adoption.

jokes.text: the fact that I'm a woman means we will no longer have any women presidents because we've already tried one and she fucking sucked.
jokes.setup: Discussing why Candi Caruso doesn't want Nevada job
jokes.punchline: Brutal self-aware assessment of Selina's presidency's impact on future women
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and self-aware about the show's central premise. Sharp political satire that cuts deep.

jokes.text: Unless afterward she went into a bathroom stall and used the old two-fingered wood chipper.
jokes.setup: Saying Candi Caruso wouldn't eat this much shit
jokes.punchline: Crude bulimia reference with vivid metaphor
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that's crafted but relies on shock value. Typical Veep pushing boundaries.

jokes.text: Wait, is that when you had your nervous breakdown? No, no, I did not... no, I went to a spa, sweetie.
jokes.setup: Selina describing going to Arizona spa after loss
jokes.punchline: Catherine casually revealing it was mental health crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy as daughter innocently exposes mother's lies about mental health crisis.

jokes.text: She's on the math team.
jokes.setup: Revealing she's in high school
jokes.punchline: Additional detail emphasizing her youth/innocence
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - the innocent detail makes Jonah's situation even more inappropriate and cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: She already told me she was a senior at Georgetown. Turns out she meant Georgetown Day School.
jokes.setup: Jonah explaining his hookup situation
jokes.punchline: Revealing she's in high school, not college
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes Jonah's situation much worse. Perfect cringe comedy timing.

jokes.text: Plus she taught me how to make sure the camera is on.
jokes.setup: Richard explaining why he's helping with documentary
jokes.punchline: Admitting he doesn't know basic camera operation
jokes.explanation: Classic Richard incompetence played for gentle laughs. Fits character perfectly but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: You listen to me, all right? You are already dead. What you do now you do for your family.
jokes.setup: Selina calling Jonah before crucial vote
jokes.punchline: Speaking to him like a doomed soldier in war movie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic escalation that treats political failure like death. Perfect Selina intensity and quotability.

jokes.text: Is my entire presidency about to have its neck snapped by Congressman Lennie here?
jokes.setup: Worried about Jonah's crucial vote
jokes.punchline: Comparing Jonah to Lennie from 'Of Mice and Men'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literary reference that perfectly captures Jonah's well-meaning but destructive nature. Highly quotable and clever.

jokes.text: It's like the best episode of 'Falcon Crest' ever.
jokes.setup: Three generations of Meyer women in Oval Office
jokes.punchline: Comparing meaningful family moment to trashy soap opera
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that deflates sentimental moment with pop culture reference typical of Selina's superficiality.

jokes.text: I do hope you'll wait till your hair grows to have your portrait painted.
jokes.setup: Visiting Oval Office for first time
jokes.punchline: Criticizing Selina's appearance instead of congratulating
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing where Selina's issues come from. Classic toxic mother behavior.

jokes.text: She looks like a boy.
jokes.setup: Continuing criticism of Selina's appearance
jokes.punchline: Blunt insult about daughter's masculinity
jokes.explanation: Effective character moment but simple insult comedy. Shows generational dysfunction clearly.

jokes.text: Jesus fucking Christ. You got to tell your sister to stop calling me.
jokes.setup: Amy asking Dan about plans
jokes.punchline: Dan's sister apparently stalking him romantically
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable comedy about inappropriate family dynamics. Fits Dan's sleazy character.

jokes.text: It actually cost the same as the kid, so it works out.
jokes.setup: Dan describing expensive home theater setup
jokes.punchline: Casually equating cost of TV with cost of child
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark comedy about commodifying children. Perfect economy and Dan's materialistic character.

jokes.text: I can't wait for Wendy to see it. She is gonna... possibly like it.
jokes.setup: Dan excited about showing Wendy the man cave
jokes.punchline: Deflating his own enthusiasm with realistic assessment
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware comedy where Dan's optimism crashes into reality mid-sentence.

jokes.text: How am I doing? Eating so much pussy I'm shitting clit, son.
jokes.setup: Jonah asked about his campaign
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely vulgar sexual boast at elementary school
jokes.explanation: Pure cringe comedy that perfectly captures Jonah's inappropriate nature, but relies heavily on shock value.

jokes.text: I think the problem is that you lack upper body strength.
jokes.setup: Jonah struggling to chop wood for campaign photo op
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of his physical inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation about Jonah's physical incompetence during staged masculinity display.

jokes.text: ♪ If you want to know who we are, We are gentlemen of Japan... ♪
jokes.setup: Talking about missing Gilbert & Sullivan show
jokes.punchline: Actually singing operetta lyrics
jokes.explanation: Charming character moment that reveals unexpected depth and passion in minor character.

jokes.text: I just thank God I didn't hit those schoolchildren. Who gives a fuck about them? Go! Go!
jokes.setup: Richard expressing relief about not hitting children
jokes.punchline: Catherine immediately dismissing concern for children's safety
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast showing Catherine's inherited ruthlessness versus Richard's basic decency.

jokes.text: Stalemate his endgame. Shades of Karpov versus Kasparov.
jokes.setup: Describing Tom's political strategy
jokes.punchline: Overintellectualizing with chess grandmaster reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent character moment showing his tendency to overcomplicate with intellectual references.

jokes.text: You big lesbo.
jokes.setup: Jonah waking up his campaign manager
jokes.punchline: Casual homophobic insult as wake-up call
jokes.explanation: Low-effort insult comedy that fits Jonah's character but lacks craft or originality.

jokes.text: Joyce, she took care of me after my third heart attack. My second wife was a nurse as well. I guess I have a thing for nurses, huh?
jokes.setup: Introducing his wife Joyce
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing pattern of marrying caregivers
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Ben's health issues and pattern of dependent relationships. Good character insight.

jokes.text: These Precious Moments figurines are a very good investment.
jokes.setup: Ben's wife showing Catherine around their home
jokes.punchline: Earnest belief in collectible figurines as investment
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Joyce's earnest middle-class values and financial naivety.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's the least reassuring sentence I've heard since 'It's okay, it's just the tip.'
jokes.setup: Responding to reassurance about winning votes
jokes.punchline: Comparing to classic sexual lie
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy that perfectly captures the falseness of political reassurances. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: It's looking tighter than Will's own butthole when he's got his finger jammed up there watching 'Bad News Bears' while he jerks off.
jokes.setup: Describing how tight the vote is
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific sexual scenario involving Will
jokes.explanation: Crude shock humor that's overly elaborate and relies on gross-out rather than craft.

jokes.text: All right, I wanted to fuck you! That's right! And now you're trying to fuck me again tonight. No, I am fucking you tonight! No, I'm gonna fuck you! No, no, no, no, no...
jokes.setup: Confrontation between Selina and Tom
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphors escalating into literal panic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing political confrontation through sexual metaphor, but relies on repetition.

jokes.text: This is Catherine Meyer, my, uh, work friend.
jokes.setup: Marjorie introducing Catherine to her conservative parents
jokes.punchline: Awkward euphemism for girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment about closeted relationships and family dynamics.

jokes.text: Marjorie is insufferable, okay? There are other... there are other fish in the sapphic sea, babe.
jokes.setup: Comforting Catherine about breakup
jokes.punchline: Adapting 'fish in the sea' for lesbian relationship
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Selina trying to be supportive while maintaining her characteristic bluntness.

jokes.text: My God, in 12 years, I'm gonna be a shriveled-up can of ass.
jokes.setup: Realizing Tom could be president for 12 years
jokes.punchline: Grotesque description of her future appearance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Selina moment - her vanity and desperation expressed through vivid, disgusting imagery. 'Can of ass' is inspired.

jokes.text: Seriously, I can't... I mean, my political window just slams shut the second I can't wear sleeveless dresses.
jokes.setup: Worrying about aging out of politics
jokes.punchline: Reducing political viability to ability to show arms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about sexism and ageism in politics delivered with perfect Selina vanity and self-awareness.

jokes.text: my political window just slams shut the second I can't wear sleeveless dresses.
jokes.setup: Continuing about aging out of politics
jokes.punchline: Specific vanity concern about arm appearance
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how Selina reduces complex political calculations to personal vanity concerns. Brutally specific and true to character.

jokes.text: Please, God, deliver Jonah to Congress and then give him any kind of cancer. I don't care.
jokes.setup: Praying for Jonah to arrive for vote
jokes.punchline: Adding casual prayer for his death
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy showing Selina's transactional relationship with both God and human life.

jokes.text: The gentleman from New Hampshire puts forth onto the floor a do-over, thank you.
jokes.setup: Jonah realizing he voted wrong
jokes.punchline: Formal parliamentary language for childish concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment mixing formal procedure with childish understanding of how government works.

jokes.text: Oh, you're clutching your bag like I'm gonna steal it.
jokes.setup: Tourist meeting Selina
jokes.punchline: Pointing out defensive body language
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that shows Selina's awareness of how people perceive her.

jokes.text: Some say a tie is like kissing your sister. But it took an electoral college tie for me to get to kiss the sister I never knew I had.
jokes.setup: Returning to opening metaphor about ties
jokes.punchline: Romantic revelation about Marjorie using same metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural callback that recontextualizes the entire episode through Catherine's personal journey.

jokes.text: Truck stop glory hole. Can do a handstand still.
jokes.setup: Describing Gary
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual insult followed by innocent physical skill
jokes.explanation: Good contrast comedy mixing crude insults with wholesome observations about Gary.

jokes.text: Sometimes, like, in a futuristic... like a sci-fi movie, you'll see, like, a robot that's like the old version of the robot and you kind of feel bad.
jokes.setup: Describing Mike
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to obsolete robot that evokes pity
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly original metaphor that captures Mike's outdated competence and inherent pathos.

jokes.text: What's the best lesbian porn site?
jokes.setup: End of documentary interviews
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate personal question to Catherine
jokes.explanation: Crude shock humor that relies on inappropriate sexual question rather than craft. Weak ending joke.

tmdb_overview: Catherine and Marjorie have a fight. Mike prepares for his babies. Selina is interviewed.
insight: Ninety-minute political chaos at 1.59 jokes-per-minute—Veep's densest cringe yet.
